text: EU-headquartered fintech. Primary ask: Private VPC deployment with strict EU-only data residency for model inputs/outputs and audit trail. Secondary: selective UK and Canada lanes for localized products (must be opt-in). Strong requirement for customer-managed KMS (HSM) and end-to-end audit logging. SSO/SAML + SCIM required. Interested in Redwood Private (VPC) with Dedicated fallback for burst. Cost sensitivity moderate — willing to commit if SLOs + residency are guaranteed. Legal pushed back on DPA wording re: cross-border transfers. Quote: 'We cannot tolerate token-level residency leakage — full chain must be auditable.'

text:
Top-line: federal homeland agency evaluating on-prem/air-gapped RAG platform for classified document search. High procurement overhead; must pass JITC-style security checklist and formal red-team. Prospect has existing on-prem ELK + custom BM25 baseline but wants embeddings + reranking for improved precision at scale.

Recent timeline:
- 2025-09-18: inbound contact from technology acquisition team (initial qualification). AE assigned (Ethan P.).
- 2025-10-02: discovery call w/ CTO office (Maya joined). Captured scale: ~1.2B documents across agencies, live ingestion from records mgmt and scanned OCR pipelines.
- 2025-12-07: architecture workshop — requirement: no outbound egress from classified network; model artifacts must be packaged, signed, and verified.
- 2026-01-19: POC scoping meeting. Agreed POC = 100M-doc index on isolated cluster, vector index + lightweight ANN, reranking mod...

text: Fabrikam is an existing Hosted API customer up for renewal in March. They anticipate a step-function increase in LLM usage due to a Q2 rollout of an AI agent inside their support product. Main renewal theme: shift from reactive pay-as-you-go to a predictable annual commit with guardrails (clear usage definitions, invoice preview, and alerts for spend/run-rate). They are receptive to adding Dedicated baseline capacity for steady traffic but want the option to burst to Hosted API without surprise bills.

title: Sable Harbor Financial Tech
preview:
Account background: midwestern regional fintech offering B2B payment rails and treasury services. Early AI usage for customer support and transaction enrichment. Very conservative security posture — must pass internal infosec and legal before procurement.

Encryption posture (customer focus):
- In-transit: require m...
text:
Account background: midwestern regional fintech offering B2B payment rails and treasury services. Early AI usage for customer support and transaction enrichment. Very conservative security posture — must pass internal infosec and legal before procurement.

Encryption posture (customer focus):
- In-transit: require mTLS for all control-plane and data-plane connections. Mandate mutual certs for any Redwood endpoint; no plain TLS only allowed. Use of client cert rotation every 30 days preferred.
- At-rest: AES-256-GCM envelope encryption across database, object storage, and ephemeral caches. Customer requires proof of encryption-at-rest for token caches and model KV caches.
- KMS integration: BYOK required for production keys. Customer prefers AWS KMS with HSM-backed key material (CloudHSM) or external HSM via AWS External Key Store. Key rotation policy: automatic rotation with 90-day ro...

title: Northbridge Analytics
preview: Primary requirement is production-safe optimization configuration changes under Redwood Optimize (batching/caching/quantization profiles). Northbridge wants config versioning, staged rollout w/ canary, and rollback that is fast and predictable. They consider this a 'change management' control (not just a product fea...
text:
Primary requirement is production-safe optimization configuration changes under Redwood Optimize (batching/caching/quantization profiles). Northbridge wants config versioning, staged rollout w/ canary, and rollback that is fast and predictable. They consider this a 'change management' control (not just a product feature) and want it documented and contractable.

Key asks from Northbridge:
- Safe apply workflow: validate config + staged rollout (by tenant/model/region) w/ optional canary percentage.
- Gating: must pause/fail rollout on elevated 5xx and p95/p99 latency; for quantization profiles, must also gate on eval/quality signals (they asked how noisy evals are handled).
- Rollback: one-click rollback in UI and API; rollback should revert active config pointer to a prior immutable version; needs clear behavior if rollout is partially applied (what does 'rolled back' mean across wor...

text:
Customer wanted turnkey voice embedding + streaming generative responses.

Economic concern: continuous 24/7 model use; downstream costs matter (transcription + tokens).

Security: wanted SOC2 + ability to restrict data retention per customer. Our private offering seen as heavy lift.

Quote from Samir: 'We need something that devs can spin up with minimal ops overhead — brand matters.'

SE note: POC exposed slightly higher p95 for short-turn interactions vs direct API baseline.

AE note: seat of influence was CTO; CTO neutral — SRE favored OpenAI for operational simplicity.

Competitive intel: openai rep provided simplified migration plan and volume discounts; influential.

No formal RFP; decision tracked as 'vendor consolidation to OpenAI'

text:
Primary workload: (1) clinician-facing summarization of long-form notes (streaming preferred), (2) internal doc-search/RAG over policies/procedures, (3) support-agent style Q&A for staff. Throughput: initial ~50–150 concurrent users, spiky during business hours; latency goal stated as “<2s time-to-first-token for typical prompts” for the summarization UI. Strong cost sensitivity for long-context summaries; asked about batching/caching and model/quantization options.

Deployment preference: Private (VPC) strongly preferred; Dedicated is a fallback only if we can document strict isolation, retention controls, and clear shared responsibility. They explicitly do not want prompts/responses retained beyond what is necessary for operations.

Security/compliance: SOC 2 Type II report required under NDA. Asked multiple times about HIPAA/BAA: want a vendor willing to sign a BAA for any workflow...

text:
Topline: large defense-tier contact center integrator. Onboarding for mission-critical 24/7 ops. Must be fully air-gapped for classification boundary; cannot allow outbound tokens.

Summary of requirements:
- Workloads: live agent assist (real-time cues), post-call ticket summarization (structured), tool-calling to case mgmt and escalation workflows, and automated surge handling during incidents.
- Perf targets: median agent assist latency <120ms for token stream (per-agent), sustained throughput 10k tokens/sec during peak events, burst to 50k tokens/sec for incident windows.
- Reliability: automatic failover to local fallback models when primary unavailable; prefer deterministic degrade to text-only (no remote fallthrough).
- Models: open-model preference for on-prem licensing; need support for quantized LLMs and ability to pin to specific model versions.
- Security: FIPS-validated c...

title: Haven Arc Trust Systems
preview:
Summary: Large defense-adjacent contact center operator evaluating Redwood Private for agent-assist and ticket summarization in a fully air-gapped environment. Primary concern is deterministic, auditable outputs and resilience during surge events.

Key requirements: minimal dependency on outbound network, ability to...
text:
Summary: Large defense-adjacent contact center operator evaluating Redwood Private for agent-assist and ticket summarization in a fully air-gapped environment. Primary concern is deterministic, auditable outputs and resilience during surge events.

Key requirements: minimal dependency on outbound network, ability to run in air-gapped on-prem datacenter, FIPS/HSM KMS support, audit log continuity, SSO via enterprise SAML, retention controls per DoD guidelines. Integration must sit in front of existing Genesys/Avaya ACD and call-routing stack; need tool-calling to surface backend actions (ticket creation, CRM lookups) without exposing external endpoints.

Performance targets discussed on 2026-01-15 call: 600-900 concurrent agents at peak; target 1st-token latency <250ms for agent-assist in most flows; summary generation SLA <500ms average; deterministic reranking for KB hits with explai...

- Account: Cedarline Systems (medical devices firmware + clinical analytics). Highly regulated (FDA) — cannot send PHI outside corporate VPC.
- Primary ask: enterprise IDE copilot with guaranteed sub-100ms token stream for in-IDE completions, ability to pin models per team, and strict audit trails.
- Prefers private control plane sitting in their AWS account (VPC endpoint), occasional on-prem for air-gapped test rigs.
- SE feedback: workload is high-throughput small-completions (code completions, signature generation, unit test scaffolding) — batching strategy must preserve low tail latency.
- Security: SSO via SAML (Okta), audit logs to their Splunk, keys in their KMS, 7-year retention on code review artifacts per compliance.
- Quote from CTO: "We need deterministic inference behavior across dev and CI — model pinning + rollout control is non-negotiable."

title: Helio Financial
preview: Enterprise requirements centered on auditability and safe operations for route-level experiments. Helio is comfortable with Redwood’s route-level A/B testing concept, but they will not run it on production payment-adjacent workflows without (1) complete audit logging for experiment lifecycle and configuration change...
text: Enterprise requirements centered on auditability and safe operations for route-level experiments. Helio is comfortable with Redwood’s route-level A/B testing concept, but they will not run it on production payment-adjacent workflows without (1) complete audit logging for experiment lifecycle and configuration changes, (2) RBAC separation of duties, and (3) export to SIEM (Splunk) with retention controls. Secondary requirements: deterministic stickiness for end-user assignment and guardrails to auto-stop/soft-stop on latency/error regressions.

- Customer since 2023 (started on hosted API for embeddings + reranking). Started POC for Dedicated in Q4-2024 based on seasonal throughput needs. Renewal for hosted contract due 2025-06 but want to fold into Dedicated reserved capacity SOW.

Primary drivers:
- Predictable throughput for peak chartering season (May-Oct).
- Strong need for regional residency (APAC) for some customers and partners.
- Cost predictability: looking to move from per-token hosted spend to committed Dedicated with Optimize to reduce unit costs.

Technical requirements / targets (from SE calls):
- Workload: real-time dispatch suggestions (chat-like), bulk document embeddings/search, post-voyage summarization (batch).
- Latency target: p95 < 300ms for short-turn dispatch queries; tail latency most important.
- Throughput: sustained ~120–250 RPS at peak across multimodel routes.
- Model prefe...

text:
Deal summary: Stalled / closed-lost due to unit economics. CinderWorks evaluated Redwood Dedicated + Private options for a fintech risk-screening stack. Primary objection = total run-rate (GPU + token) and size/duration of commit required to reach the quoted unit price. Selected competitor in late-Feb (ModelServeCo) citing lower committed GPU rates and shorter term trial.

Timeline & activity (shorthand):
- 2025-01-19: Intro call (AE Ethan Park, SE Rina Desai). Captured high-level requirements: 200 qps ingest, 150ms P95 latency, sensitive PII, EU data residency for a subset. (Gmail: gt_20250119_3312)
- 2025-01-28: Security kickoff w/ infra + sec. Request for KMS/HSM and SOC2 evidence. Fireflies ff_20250128_4421.
- 2025-02-03: Pricing review — provided Dedicated sizing options (4xA100 & 8xA100 sim) and Private deployment estimates. Finance asked for 12-month commitment scenarios + mont...

title: Fountwell Research
preview: POC summary: building a RAG-powered knowledge portal for sell-side research and internal desk research. Primary pipeline: nightly ingestion of PDFs/earnings_transcripts + SEC filings -> paragraph-level passages -> embeddings store -> retrieve+rerank. Index size target: ~120M passages (50-70M docs collapsed to passag...
text:
POC summary: building a RAG-powered knowledge portal for sell-side research and internal desk research. Primary pipeline: nightly ingestion of PDFs/earnings_transcripts + SEC filings -> paragraph-level passages -> embeddings store -> retrieve+rerank. Index size target: ~120M passages (50-70M docs collapsed to passages). Latency/throughput needs: interactive search for analysts (p95 < 120ms on rerank path), bursty workloads during open/close with sustained 400-600 QPS and peaks to 1k QPS. Cost sensitivity: procurement asked for unit economics — target $0.005-0.02 per query depending on one vs two-stage rerank. Security/residency: data must stay in NA region, customer-managed KMS keys required, SOC2 + audit logging mandatory. Preferred deployment: Dedicated pools in VPC; evaluating private/air-gapped on prem for regulated clients.

Key quotes: "We can't accept any residency leakage — ke...

text:
Status: Closed - lost to hyperscaler managed endpoints (AWS).

Summary bullets:
- Late-stage procurement; heavy emphasis from procurement and infra on reducing vendor footprint.
- POC (embeddings + semantic search) ran on Redwood Dedicated in a single VPC subnet (POC cluster spun for 2 weeks). Latency and relevance metrics met internal SLOs.
- Cost model: Redwood estimated ~30-40% TCO advantage at forecasted scale (with caching + batching) but procurement prioritized single-cloud consolidation and integrated billing.
- Security review: Redwood passed technical answers; security team flagged support burden of a second inference vendor despite VPC option. Quote from SecOps: "we can't add another inference vendor into our stack right now — enterprise AWS contract covers managed endpoints and support." 
- Decision: Oakridge procurement selected AWS-managed endpoints (SageMaker/Bedrock) to...

title: Palisade Government Knowledge Systems
preview:
Summary: Palisade is a homeland-focused contractor building an enterprise search product for state and federal agencies. Primary ask: high-quality RAG for classified/unclassified stores with deterministic latency and provable data isolation.

Key constraints and asks (shorthand):
- Must run in air-gapped on-prem dat...
text:
Summary: Palisade is a homeland-focused contractor building an enterprise search product for state and federal agencies. Primary ask: high-quality RAG for classified/unclassified stores with deterministic latency and provable data isolation.

Key constraints and asks (shorthand):
- Must run in air-gapped on-prem datacenters behind agency firewall; control plane can be private (customer-managed) or hosted in gov-cloud if required.
- Indexing scale: plan to onboard ~30M documents initially, expected growth to 100M+ within 18 months. Concerned about embedding generation cost and storage for dense vectors.
- Latency SLOs: 95th percentile under 300ms for typical query (embedding + rerank), target p50 < 120ms. Most queries are short chat-like queries and doc retrieval.
- Cost sensitivity: procurement expects unit economics analysis (cost/query) for hosted vs dedicated vs private. Requested ...

title: Auriga FinServ Intelligence Hub
preview: Key account for Q1 push — R&D + Investment Research teams consolidated 3 legacy indices. Preference for dedicated capacity with private control plane. Primary ask: scale to ~30M docs (mixed PDFs, time-series tables) with sub-400ms 95th-percentile rerank latency. Cost sensitivity: goal ~<$0.002/query for vector + rer...
text:
Key account for Q1 push — R&D + Investment Research teams consolidated 3 legacy indices. Preference for dedicated capacity with private control plane. Primary ask: scale to ~30M docs (mixed PDFs, time-series tables) with sub-400ms 95th-percentile rerank latency. Cost sensitivity: goal ~<$0.002/query for vector + rerank on average workload.

Meeting snippets:
- "We need deterministic latency across European trading hours — spikes during market open are non-negotiable."
- Engineer: "want to avoid per-request cold starts; prefix/KV cache critical".

They are evaluating Redwood for ability to: (1) host embeddings at scale with incremental indexing; (2) run a dense-retrieval + cross-encoder rerank pipeline; (3) integrate KMS/HSM and meet EU residency + retention policy. CSM call 2025-01-20 confirmed can move to procurement if SOW matches latency SLA and KMS certs.

title: Astraeus Realtime Labs
preview:
Overview: enterprise quant trading shop building low-latency signal layer. Team: CTO (Victor Hale), Head of Infra (Rina Patel). We kicked off Dedicated evaluation 2024-09.

Key ask: deterministic tail latency <6ms p50/p95 for short completion paths; batchable work for embeddings & rerank off-path. Heavy throughput d...
text:
Overview: enterprise quant trading shop building low-latency signal layer. Team: CTO (Victor Hale), Head of Infra (Rina Patel). We kicked off Dedicated evaluation 2024-09.

Key ask: deterministic tail latency <6ms p50/p95 for short completion paths; batchable work for embeddings & rerank off-path. Heavy throughput during market open windows (13:30-14:30 ET) - want reserved capacity to avoid noisy-neighbor impact.

SLA negotiation highlights: they insist on financial credits for downtime (tiered), 99.95% as baseline target proposed by them, pushing for 99.99% for core inference path. Must have <1 minute failover to fallback model variant. Require 72-hour maintenance notice, 2x per month maintenance window only outside 13:00-15:00 ET.

Security/compliance: strict KMS for keys, audit log retention 2 years, SSO via SAML + SCIM sync. Data residency: logs to remain in us-east region only. L...
